failure_story: The committee treated management's quarterly liquidation-basis net asset estimate ($11.79 per share as of 2026-03-31) as near-contracted shareholder cash on a 2.46-year path to December 31, 2028. The Article 78 appeal on the Flowerfield subdivision was lost, forcing a redesign that voided or delayed the signed B2K land-sale contract. Smithtown site-plan approval never arrived on the assumed schedule. Entitlement, legal, and administrative burn continued while rental receipts fell short of forecasts. Management raised dilutive capital through the existing shelf registration to fund operations past the 2028 target, and the liquidation timeline slipped again. Net assets in liquidation were re-estimated downward for a second consecutive cycle (after the prior year's drop from $13.91 to $11.76 per share). Realized distributions fell well below the base-case payoff, the market discount never closed on roughly 2,000 to 6,000 shares traded daily, and capital was trapped in an illiquid micro-cap with a negative total return despite the disclosed NAV story.
earliest_warning_signals: Article 78 appeal (Incorporated Village of Head of the Harbor v. Town of Smithtown) remained pending as of the 10-Q filed 2026-05-13, and the company stated Flowerfield would not be sold until it was resolved.
Net assets in liquidation per share had already fallen about 15% in one year, from $13.91 (12/31/2024) to $11.76 (12/31/2025), as the target completion date was extended and cost estimates rose.
The company had extended its liquidation target twice already (originally end of 2026, then 2028), signaling schedule risk before any new capital was committed.
B2K site-plan approval was still outstanding at packet freeze; management expected Q3 2026 approval but the base NAV already embedded the $28.74 million high-end B2K price.
Management disclosed in the 10-K FY2025 MD&A that additional capital may be needed through 2028, a direct dilution risk to per-share payoff.
The 2021 mortgage loan with Rialto Capital matured October 10, 2026 with only an extension option, and the refinanced 2023 LLYR facility carried a 15% rate as of January 2026.
Daily trading volume was often only a few thousand shares, so building or exiting a position near the quoted price was structurally difficult.
The three named debt facilities did not fully reconcile to the $10,790,194 loans-payable total in the available filing extract, leaving leverage and maturity exposure under-tested.
forensic_checks: Track the Article 78 docket and any subsequent zoning or subdivision filings for an adverse ruling or forced redesign of the Flowerfield eight-lot plan.
Obtain Smithtown Planning Department records on the B2K 49-acre site-plan submission and compare approval timing to the B2K agreement's subdivision and closing contingencies.
Reconcile the original bank line, 2021 Rialto mortgage, and 2023 LLYR mortgage footnotes to the $10,790,194 balance-sheet total and stress refinancing at or before the October 2026 maturity.
Monitor each quarterly liquidation-basis re-estimation in 10-Q and 10-K filings for downward revisions to real-estate net realizable value, remaining cost accruals, or target dissolution dates.
Verify whether the B2K contract terminated, closed at the $24.0 million low end, or survived entitlement changes; quantify per-share impact versus the $28.74 million already in base NRV.
Scan 8-K and prospectus filings (424B5 shelf registration on file) for dilutive equity raises, rights offerings, or loan amendments that shrink net assets in liquidation per share.
Confirm no interim distributions were assumed in the base payoff and that cash burn matched the $16,709,887 estimated remaining liquidation and operating costs net of receipts.
Review Star Equity Fund LP standstill expiration (December 31, 2026, or 2027 under stated conditions) for renewed activism that accelerates a below-NRV sale.
Compare Cortlandt Manor subdivision progress against the 2027 approval assumption and test whether the 2014 appraised-value price floor still binds if timelines slip further.
unresolved_items: Article 78 appeal outcome was unknown at packet freeze; an adverse ruling is the single largest swing factor between base NAV and a materially lower realized payoff.
B2K site-plan status with the Town of Smithtown was unconfirmed at commit date despite the base case already booking the high-end contract value.
Debt facility line items did not fully reconcile to the consolidated loans-payable total, leaving maturity, rate, and covenant exposure partially untested.
No approved or pending third-party research was indexed; the thesis rested entirely on primary filings with no external blend or independent NAV cross-check.
No public short-seller report was located, but the adversarial scan noted that a name this small and thinly traded could still carry undisclosed short interest without a published bear case.
Human sign-off on stance and litigation status was still open; mechanical tools proposed accumulate while Marvin recommended hold pending appeal and site-plan resolution.
Whether management would execute a dilutive capital raise before asset sales completed remained a disclosed possibility, not a modeled probability in the base payoff path.
